title: coulterina oregona
intro: Coulterina oregona, also known as Coulter's California currant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Grossulariaceae. It is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for its edible berries and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, dysentery, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Coulterina oregona can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting digestive health.
